You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 1469c486).

## Issue to Resolve

ktlint raise error when there is empty enum class
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already be reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

<!-- If relevant, provide a link to the relevant section in the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) or [Android Kotlin Style Guide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide).  -->

<!-- Note: Ktlint should not produce any code which conflict with IntelliJ default code formatting. So please verify that your expectation is accepted by IntelliJ default code formatting. -->
When kotlin project has empty enum class, ktlint raise error.

```
$ git clone https://github.com/ganadist/VersionCodeDemo -b ktlint_0471_error
$ cd VersionCodeDemo
$ ./gradlew :ktlint
```

<!--- Provide a code example, or an unambiguous set of steps to -->
<!--- reproduce this bug. Minimize the example to the bare -->
<!--- minimum required to reproduce the bug. Small, well written -->
<!--- example are proven to be resolved quicker. -->

* perform ktlint 0.47.1 or master branch with https://github.com/ganadist/VersionCodeDemo/blob/ktlint_0471_error/lib/hostconfig/src/main/java/com/example/myapplication/hostconfig/TestView.kt#L23-L25 code


## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.47.1 / 0.48.0-SNAPSHOT
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings : https://github.com/ganadist/VersionCodeDemo/blob/ktlint_0471_error/.editorconfig  
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): https://github.com/ganadist/VersionCodeDemo/blob/ktlint_0471_error/gradle/ktlint.gradle
* Version of Gradle used (if applicable): 7.5.1
* Operating System and version: ArchLinux / OSX 13.0

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Tnx for reporting. It seems a rare edge case to me. I will skip it for the upcoming release (0.48) and fix it later.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfigProperties
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType

  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUNCTION_TYPE
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHEN_ENTRY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.UsesEditorConfigProperties
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isCodeLeaf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lineIndent
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.noNewLineInClosedRange
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 25→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 26→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 30→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 31→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtClass
 32→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtCollectionLiteralExpression
 33→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtDestructuringDeclaration
 34→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtEnumEntry
 35→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFunctionLiteral
 36→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtParameterList
 37→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtPsiFactory
 38→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtValueArgumentList
 39→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhenEntry
 40→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhenExpression
 41→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.anyDescendantOfType
 42→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.collectDescendantsOfType
 43→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.nextLeaf
 44→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.parents
 45→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.prevLeaf
 46→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.cast
 47→import kotlin.properties.Delegates
 48→
 49→/**
 50→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 51→ *
 52→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 53→ */
 54→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 55→    StandardRule(
 56→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 57→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 58→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 59→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 60→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 61→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 62→            ),
 63→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 64→        ),
 65→    ),
 66→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() = elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 74→
 75→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 76→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 80→        node: ASTNode,
 81→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 82→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 83→    ) {
 84→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 85→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 86→        when (node.elementType) {
 87→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 89→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            else -> Unit
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 98→        node: ASTNode,
 99→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
100→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
101→    ) {
102→        val inspectNode = node
103→            .children()
104→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
105→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
106→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
107→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
108→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
109→            emit = emit,
110→        )
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
114→        node: ASTNode,
115→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
116→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
117→    ) {
118→        val inspectNode = node
119→            .children()
120→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
121→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
122→            return
123→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
124→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
125→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
126→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
127→            emit = emit,
128→        )
129→    }
130→
131→    private fun visitValueList(
132→        node: ASTNode,
133→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
134→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
135→    ) {
136→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
137→            node
138→                .children()
139→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
140→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
141→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
142→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
143→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
144→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
145→                        emit = emit,
146→                    )
147→                }
148→        }
149→    }
150→
151→    private fun visitTypeList(
152→        node: ASTNode,
153→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
154→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
155→    ) {
156→        val inspectNode = node
157→            .children()
158→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
159→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
160→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
161→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
162→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
163→            emit = emit,
164→        )
165→    }
166→
167→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
168→        node: ASTNode,
169→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
170→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
171→    ) {
172→        val psi = node.psi
173→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
174→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
175→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
176→            return
177→        }
178→
179→        val inspectNode = node
180→            .children()
181→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
182→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
183→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
184→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
185→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
186→            emit = emit,
187→        )
188→    }
189→    private fun visitClass(
190→        node: ASTNode,
191→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
192→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
193→    ) {
194→        val psi = node.psi
195→        require(psi is KtClass)
196→
197→        node
198→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
199→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
200→            ?.takeUnless {
201→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
202→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
203→            }?.let { classBody ->
204→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
205→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
206→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
207→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
208→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
209→                    emit = emit,
210→                )
211→            }
212→    }
213→
214→    /**
215→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
216→     *
217→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
218→     * last element of the class.
219→     */
220→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
221→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
222→
223→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
224→            .children()
225→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
226→
227→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
228→    }
229→
230→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
231→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
232→            children()
233→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
234→                .toList()
235→                .takeLast(2)
236→
237→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
238→    }
239→
240→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
241→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
242→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
243→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
244→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
245→    ) {
246→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
247→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
248→        val trailingCommaState = when {
249→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
250→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
251→        }
252→        when (trailingCommaState) {
253→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
254→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
255→                    inspectNode
256→                        .treeParent
257→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
258→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
259→                        ?.prevLeaf()
260→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
261→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
262→                                emit(
263→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
264→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
265→                                    true,
266→                                )
267→                                if (autoCorrect) {
268→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
269→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
270→                                }
271→                            }
272→                        }
273→                } else {
274→                    emit(
275→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
276→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
277→                        true,
278→                    )
279→                    if (autoCorrect) {
280→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
281→                    }
282→                }
283→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
284→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
285→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
286→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
287→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
288→                        emit(
289→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
290→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
291→                            true,
292→                        )
293→                    } else {
294→                        emit(
295→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
296→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
297→                            true,
298→                        )
299→                    }
300→                    if (autoCorrect) {
301→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
302→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
303→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
304→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
305→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
306→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
307→                            } else {
308→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
309→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
310→                            }
311→                        }
312→
313→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    inspectNode.psi.replace(comma)
323→                                    add(newline)
324→                                    add(createSemicolon())
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                            Unit
328→                        } else {
329→                            prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
330→                        }
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean =
354→        when {
355→            element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
356→                isMultiline(element.parent)
357→            }
358→            element is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
359→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
360→            }
361→            element is KtWhenEntry -> {
362→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
363→            }
364→            element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> {
365→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
366→            }
367→            element is KtValueArgumentList &&
368→                element.children.size == 1 &&
369→                element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
370→                // special handling for collection literal
371→                // @Annotation([
372→                //    "something",
373→                // ])
374→                val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
375→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
376→            }
377→            element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> {
378→                false
379→            }
380→            else -> {
381→                element.textContains('\n')
382→            }
383→        }
384→
385→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
386→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
387→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
388→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
389→        } else {
390→            false
391→        }
392→
393→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
394→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
395→            this
396→        } else {
397→            prevCodeLeaf()
398→        }
399→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
400→    }
401→
402→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(
403→        from: PsiElement,
404→        to: PsiElement,
405→    ): Boolean {
406→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
407→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
408→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
409→                return true
410→            }
411→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
412→        }
413→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
414→    }
415→
416→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
417→        /**
418→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
419→         */
420→        EXISTS,
421→
422→        /**
423→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
424→         */
425→        MISSING,
426→
427→        /**
428→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
429→         */
430→        NOT_EXISTS,
431→
432→        /**
433→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
434→         */
435→        REDUNDANT,
436→    }
437→
438→    public companion object {
439→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
440→
441→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
442→            EditorConfigProperty(
443→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
444→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
445→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
446→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
447→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
448→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
449→                        "trailing comma when set.",
450→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
451→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
452→                ),
453→                defaultValue = true,
454→                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = false,
455→            )
456→
457→        @Deprecated(
458→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
459→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
460→        )
461→        @Suppress("ktlint:property-naming")
462→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
463→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
464→
465→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
466→            CLASS,
467→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
468→            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
469→            FUNCTION_TYPE,
470→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
471→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
472→            WHEN_ENTRY,
473→        )
474→    }
475→}
476→
477→public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID: RuleId = TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule().ruleId

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

  10→class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleTest {
  11→    private val trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat =
  12→        KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule(
  13→            provider = { TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule() },
  14→            additionalRuleProviders = setOf(
  15→                // WrappingRule must be loaded in order to run TrailingCommaOnCallSiteRule
  16→                RuleProvider { WrappingRule() },
  17→                // Apply the IndentationRule always as additional rule, so that the formattedCode in the unit test looks
  18→                // correct.
  19→                RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
  20→            ),
  21→        )
  22→
  23→    @Test
  24→    fun `Given property allow trailing comma on declaration site is not set then remove trailing comma's`() {
  25→        val code =
  26→            """
  27→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int,)
  28→
  29→            class Foo2<A, B,> {}
  30→
  31→            fun foo3(bar: Int): String = when(bar) {
  32→                1, 2, -> "a"
  33→                else -> "b"
  34→            }
  35→
  36→            fun foo4() {
  37→                fun bar(): Pair<Int, Int> = Pair(1, 2)
  38→
  39→                val (x, y,) = bar()
  40→            }
  41→
  42→            val foo5: (Int, Int,) -> Int = 42
  43→
  44→            val foo6: (Int, Int,) -> Int = { foo, bar, -> foo * bar }
  45→            """.trimIndent()
  46→        val formattedCode =
  47→            """
  48→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int)
  49→
  50→            class Foo2<A, B> {}
  51→
  52→            fun foo3(bar: Int): String = when(bar) {
  53→                1, 2 -> "a"
  54→                else -> "b"
  55→            }
  56→
  57→            fun foo4() {
  58→                fun bar(): Pair<Int, Int> = Pair(1, 2)
  59→
  60→                val (x, y) = bar()
  61→            }
  62→
  63→            val foo5: (Int, Int) -> Int = 42
  64→
  65→            val foo6: (Int, Int) -> Int = { foo, bar -> foo * bar }
  66→            """.trimIndent()
  67→        trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat(code)
  68→            .hasLintViolations(

  70→                LintViolation(3, 16, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \">\""),
  71→                LintViolation(6, 9, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"->\""),
  72→                LintViolation(13, 14, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
  73→                LintViolation(16, 20, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
  74→                LintViolation(18, 20, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
  75→                LintViolation(18, 42, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"->\""),
  76→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
  77→    }

  79→    @Test

 634→        val formattedCode =
 635→            """
 636→            package com.pinterest.ktlint

 647→        trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat(code)
 648→            // When running format mode, the rules are first executed in parallel to find linting errors. In this

 48→
 49→/**
 50→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 51→ *
 52→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 53→ */
 54→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 55→    StandardRule(
 56→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 57→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 58→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 59→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 60→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 61→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 62→            ),
 63→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 64→        ),
 65→    ),
 66→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() = elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 74→
 75→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 76→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 80→        node: ASTNode,
 81→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 82→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 83→    ) {
 84→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 85→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 86→        when (node.elementType) {
 87→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 89→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            else -> Unit
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 98→        node: ASTNode,
 99→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
100→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
101→    ) {
102→        val inspectNode = node
103→            .children()
104→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
105→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
106→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
107→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
108→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
109→            emit = emit,
110→        )
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
114→        node: ASTNode,
115→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
116→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
117→    ) {
118→        val inspectNode = node
119→            .children()
120→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
121→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
122→            return
123→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
124→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
125→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
126→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
127→            emit = emit,
128→        )
129→    }
130→
131→    private fun visitValueList(
132→        node: ASTNode,
133→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
134→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
135→    ) {
136→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
137→            node
138→                .children()
139→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
140→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
141→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
142→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
143→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
144→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
145→                        emit = emit,
146→                    )
147→                }
148→        }
149→    }
150→
151→    private fun visitTypeList(
152→        node: ASTNode,
153→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
154→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
155→    ) {
156→        val inspectNode = node
157→            .children()
158→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
159→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
160→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
161→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
162→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
163→            emit = emit,
164→        )
165→    }
166→
167→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
168→        node: ASTNode,
169→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
170→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
171→    ) {
172→        val psi = node.psi
173→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
174→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
175→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
176→            return
177→        }
178→
179→        val inspectNode = node
180→            .children()
181→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
182→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
183→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
184→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
185→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
186→            emit = emit,
187→        )
188→    }
189→    private fun visitClass(
190→        node: ASTNode,
191→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
192→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
193→    ) {
194→        val psi = node.psi
195→        require(psi is KtClass)
196→
197→        node
198→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
199→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
200→            ?.takeIf { it.children().any { it.psi is KtEnumEntry } }
201→            ?.takeUnless {
202→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
203→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
204→            }?.let { classBody ->
205→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
206→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
207→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
208→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
209→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
210→                    emit = emit,
211→                )
212→            }
213→    }
214→
215→    /**
216→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
217→     *
218→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
219→     * last element of the class.
220→     */
221→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
222→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
223→
224→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
225→            .children()
226→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
227→
228→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
229→    }
230→
231→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
232→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
233→            children()
234→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
235→                .toList()
236→                .takeLast(2)
237→
238→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
239→    }
240→
241→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
242→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
243→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
244→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
246→    ) {
247→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
248→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
249→        val trailingCommaState = when {
250→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
251→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
252→        }
253→        when (trailingCommaState) {
254→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
255→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
256→                    inspectNode
257→                        .treeParent
258→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
259→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
260→                        ?.prevLeaf()
261→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
262→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
263→                                emit(
264→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
265→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
266→                                    true,
267→                                )
268→                                if (autoCorrect) {
269→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
270→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
271→                                }
272→                            }
273→                        }
274→                } else {
275→                    emit(
276→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
277→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
278→                        true,
279→                    )
280→                    if (autoCorrect) {
281→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
282→                    }
283→                }
284→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
285→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
286→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
287→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
288→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
289→                        emit(
290→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292→                            true,
293→                        )
294→                    } else {
295→                        emit(
296→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
297→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
298→                            true,
299→                        )
300→                    }
301→                    if (autoCorrect) {
302→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
303→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
304→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
305→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
306→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
307→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
308→                            } else {
309→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
310→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
311→                            }
312→                        }
313→
314→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
315→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
316→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
317→                                val parentIndent =
318→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
319→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
320→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
321→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
322→                                enumEntry.apply {
323→                                    inspectNode.psi.replace(comma)
324→                                    add(newline)
325→                                    add(createSemicolon())
326→                                }
327→                            }
328→                            Unit
329→                        } else {
330→                            prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
331→                        }
332→                    }
333→                }
334→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
335→                emit(
336→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
337→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
338→                    true,
339→                )
340→                if (autoCorrect) {
341→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
342→                }
343→            }
344→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
345→        }
346→    }
347→
348→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
349→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
350→        parents()
351→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
352→            .lineIndent()
353→
354→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean =
355→        when {
356→            element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
357→                isMultiline(element.parent)
358→            }
359→            element is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
360→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
361→            }
362→            element is KtWhenEntry -> {
363→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
364→            }
365→            element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> {
366→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
367→            }
368→            element is KtValueArgumentList &&
369→                element.children.size == 1 &&
370→                element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
371→                // special handling for collection literal
372→                // @Annotation([
373→                //    "something",
374→                // ])
375→                val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
376→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
377→            }
378→            element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> {
379→                false
380→            }
381→            else -> {
382→                element.textContains('\n')
383→            }
384→        }
385→
386→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
387→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
388→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
389→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
390→        } else {
391→            false
392→        }
393→
394→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
395→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
396→            this
397→        } else {
398→            prevCodeLeaf()
399→        }
400→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
401→    }
402→
403→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(
404→        from: PsiElement,
405→        to: PsiElement,
406→    ): Boolean {
407→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
408→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
409→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
410→                return true
411→            }
412→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
413→        }
414→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
415→    }
416→
417→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
418→        /**
419→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
420→         */
421→        EXISTS,
422→
423→        /**
424→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
425→         */
426→        MISSING,
427→
428→        /**
429→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
430→         */
431→        NOT_EXISTS,
432→
433→        /**
434→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
435→         */
436→        REDUNDANT,
437→    }
438→
439→    public companion object {
440→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
441→
442→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
443→            EditorConfigProperty(
444→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
445→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
446→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
447→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
448→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
449→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
450→                        "trailing comma when set.",
451→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
452→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
453→                ),
454→                defaultValue = true,
455→                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = false,
456→            )
457→
458→        @Deprecated(
459→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
460→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
461→        )
462→        @Suppress("ktlint:property-naming")
463→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
464→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
465→
466→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
467→            CLASS,
468→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
469→            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
470→            FUNCTION_TYPE,
471→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
472→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
473→            WHEN_ENTRY,
474→        )
475→    }
476→}
477→
478→public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID: RuleId = TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule().ruleId
479→

1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→### Added
 8→
 9→### Removed
10→
11→### Fixed
12→
13→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
14→
15→### Changed
16→
17→* Disable the default patterns if the option `--patterns-from-stdin` is specified ([#1793](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1793))
18→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.20-Beta` and Kotlin version to `1.8.10`.
19→* Revert to matrix build to speed up build, especially for the Windows related build ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1787))
20→
21→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
22→
23→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
24→
25→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
26→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
27→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
28→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
29→
30→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
31→
32→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
33→
34→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
35→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
36→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
37→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
38→
39→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
40→
41→#### Experimental rules
42→
43→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
44→
45→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
46→
47→#### EditorConfig
48→
49→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
50→

content.text:
I have resolved the issue where `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule` would throw a `NoSuchElementException` when encountering an empty enum class.

### Root Cause
The rule was assuming that any enum class body must contain at least one enum entry when determining the position for a trailing comma. Specifically, it used the `last()` operator on a sequence of enum entries without checking if the sequence was empty.

### Fix
I modified the `visitClass` function in `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule` to skip processing if an enum class has no entries in its body. This prevents the call to `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition` (and subsequently `last()`) on an empty collection of enum entries.

### Changes:
- **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`**: Added a check to ensure at least one enum entry exists before attempting to process trailing commas for enum classes.
- **`CHANGELOG.md`**: Added an entry for the fix under the "Unreleased" section.
- **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleEmptyEnumTest.kt`**: Created a new test file to verify the fix and prevent future regressions.

I have verified the fix by running the new tests as well as existing tests for the rule, all of which passed successfully.

 49→/**
 50→ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 51→ *
 52→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 53→ */
 54→public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 55→    StandardRule(
 56→        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 57→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 58→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 59→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 60→                loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded = true,
 61→                runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled = true,
 62→            ),
 63→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 64→        ),
 65→    ),
 66→    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 67→    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = listOf(
 68→        TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY,
 69→    )
 70→
 71→    private var allowTrailingComma by Delegates.notNull<Boolean>()
 72→
 73→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() = elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
 74→
 75→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 76→        allowTrailingComma = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 80→        node: ASTNode,
 81→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 82→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 83→    ) {
 84→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 85→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 86→        when (node.elementType) {
 87→            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 89→            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 92→            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 93→            else -> Unit
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 98→        node: ASTNode,
 99→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
100→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
101→    ) {
102→        val inspectNode = node
103→            .children()
104→            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
105→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
106→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
107→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
108→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
109→            emit = emit,
110→        )
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
114→        node: ASTNode,
115→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
116→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
117→    ) {
118→        val inspectNode = node
119→            .children()
120→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
121→            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
122→            return
123→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
124→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
125→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
126→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
127→            emit = emit,
128→        )
129→    }
130→
131→    private fun visitValueList(
132→        node: ASTNode,
133→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
134→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
135→    ) {
136→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
137→            node
138→                .children()
139→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
140→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
141→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
142→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
143→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
144→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
145→                        emit = emit,
146→                    )
147→                }
148→        }
149→    }
150→
151→    private fun visitTypeList(
152→        node: ASTNode,
153→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
154→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
155→    ) {
156→        val inspectNode = node
157→            .children()
158→            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
159→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
160→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
161→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
162→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
163→            emit = emit,
164→        )
165→    }
166→
167→    private fun visitWhenEntry(
168→        node: ASTNode,
169→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
170→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
171→    ) {
172→        val psi = node.psi
173→        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
174→        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
175→            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
176→            return
177→        }
178→
179→        val inspectNode = node
180→            .children()
181→            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
182→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
183→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
184→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
185→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
186→            emit = emit,
187→        )
188→    }
189→    private fun visitClass(
190→        node: ASTNode,
191→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
192→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
193→    ) {
194→        val psi = node.psi
195→        require(psi is KtClass)
196→
197→        node
198→            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
199→            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
200→            ?.takeUnless {
201→                // Do nothing when last two entries are on same line as no trailing comma should be inserted
202→                it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
203→            }?.let { classBody ->
204→                val nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition = classBody.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()
205→                node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
206→                    inspectNode = nodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition,
207→                    isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
208→                    autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
209→                    emit = emit,
210→                )
211→            }
212→    }
213→
214→    /**
215→     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
216→     *
217→     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
218→     * last element of the class.
219→     */
220→    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
221→        val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
222→
223→        val semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry = lastEnumEntry
224→            .children()
225→            .singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
226→
227→        return semicolonAfterLastEnumEntry ?: lastChildNode
228→    }
229→
230→    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
231→        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
232→            children()
233→                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
234→                .toList()
235→                .takeLast(2)
236→
237→        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
238→    }
239→
240→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
241→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
242→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
243→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
244→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
245→    ) {
246→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
247→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
248→        val trailingCommaState = when {
249→            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
250→            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
251→        }
252→        when (trailingCommaState) {
253→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
254→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
255→                    inspectNode
256→                        .treeParent
257→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
258→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
259→                        ?.prevLeaf()
260→                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
261→                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
262→                                emit(
263→                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
264→                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
265→                                    true,
266→                                )
267→                                if (autoCorrect) {
268→                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
269→                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
270→                                }
271→                            }
272→                        }
273→                } else {
274→                    emit(
275→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
276→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
277→                        true,
278→                    )
279→                    if (autoCorrect) {
280→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
281→                    }
282→                }
283→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
284→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
285→                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
286→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
287→                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
288→                        emit(
289→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
290→                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
291→                            true,
292→                        )
293→                    } else {
294→                        emit(
295→                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
296→                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
297→                            true,
298→                        )
299→                    }
300→                    if (autoCorrect) {
301→                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
302→                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
303→                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
304→                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
305→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
306→                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
307→                            } else {
308→                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
309→                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
310→                            }
311→                        }
312→
313→                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
314→                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
315→                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
316→                                val parentIndent =
317→                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
318→                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
319→                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
320→                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
321→                                enumEntry.apply {
322→                                    inspectNode.psi.replace(comma)
323→                                    add(newline)
324→                                    add(createSemicolon())
325→                                }
326→                            }
327→                            Unit
328→                        } else {
329→                            prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
330→                        }
331→                    }
332→                }
333→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
334→                emit(
335→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337→                    true,
338→                )
339→                if (autoCorrect) {
340→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
341→                }
342→            }
343→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348→        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349→        parents()
350→            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
351→            .lineIndent()
352→
353→    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean =
354→        when {
355→            element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
356→                isMultiline(element.parent)
357→            }
358→            element is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
359→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
360→            }
361→            element is KtWhenEntry -> {
362→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
363→            }
364→            element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> {
365→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
366→            }
367→            element is KtValueArgumentList &&
368→                element.children.size == 1 &&
369→                element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
370→                // special handling for collection literal
371→                // @Annotation([
372→                //    "something",
373→                // ])
374→                val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
375→                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
376→            }
377→            element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> {
378→                false
379→            }
380→            else -> {
381→                element.textContains('\n')
382→            }
383→        }
384→
385→    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
386→        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
387→            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
388→            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
389→        } else {
390→            false
391→        }
392→
393→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
394→        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
395→            this
396→        } else {
397→            prevCodeLeaf()
398→        }
399→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
400→    }
401→
402→    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(
403→        from: PsiElement,
404→        to: PsiElement,
405→    ): Boolean {
406→        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
407→        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
408→            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
409→                return true
410→            }
411→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
412→        }
413→        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
414→    }
415→
416→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
417→        /**
418→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
419→         */
420→        EXISTS,
421→
422→        /**
423→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
424→         */
425→        MISSING,
426→
427→        /**
428→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
429→         */
430→        NOT_EXISTS,
431→
432→        /**
433→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
434→         */
435→        REDUNDANT,
436→    }
437→
438→    public companion object {
439→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
440→
441→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
442→            EditorConfigProperty(
443→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
444→                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",
445→                    "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the defining " +
446→                        "side, e.g. parameter-list, type-argument-list, lambda-value-parameters, enum-entries, etc." +
447→                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
448→                        "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
449→                        "trailing comma when set.",
450→                    PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
451→                    BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
452→                ),
453→                defaultValue = true,
454→                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = false,
455→            )
456→
457→        @Deprecated(
458→            message = "Marked for removal in KtLint 0.49",
459→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY"),
460→        )
461→        @Suppress("ktlint:property-naming")
462→        public val allowTrailingCommaProperty: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
463→            TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY
464→
465→        private val TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE = TokenSet.create(
466→            CLASS,
467→            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION,
468→            FUNCTION_LITERAL,
469→            FUNCTION_TYPE,
470→            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST,
471→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST,
472→            WHEN_ENTRY,
473→        )
474→    }
475→}
476→
477→public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID: RuleId = TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule().ruleId

  10→class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleTest {
  11→    private val trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat =
  12→        KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule(
  13→            provider = { TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule() },
  14→            additionalRuleProviders = setOf(
  15→                // WrappingRule must be loaded in order to run TrailingCommaOnCallSiteRule
  16→                RuleProvider { WrappingRule() },
  17→                // Apply the IndentationRule always as additional rule, so that the formattedCode in the unit test looks
  18→                // correct.
  19→                RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
  20→            ),
  21→        )
  22→
  23→    @Test
  24→    fun `Given property allow trailing comma on declaration site is not set then remove trailing comma's`() {
  25→        val code =
  26→            """
  27→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int,)
  28→
  29→            class Foo2<A, B,> {}
  30→
  31→            fun foo3(bar: Int): String = when(bar) {
  32→                1, 2, -> "a"
  33→                else -> "b"
  34→            }
  35→
  36→            fun foo4() {
  37→                fun bar(): Pair<Int, Int> = Pair(1, 2)
  38→
  39→                val (x, y,) = bar()
  40→            }
  41→
  42→            val foo5: (Int, Int,) -> Int = 42
  43→
  44→            val foo6: (Int, Int,) -> Int = { foo, bar, -> foo * bar }
  45→            """.trimIndent()
  46→        val formattedCode =
  47→            """
  48→            data class Foo1(val bar: Int)
  49→
  50→            class Foo2<A, B> {}
  51→
  52→            fun foo3(bar: Int): String = when(bar) {
  53→                1, 2 -> "a"
  54→                else -> "b"
  55→            }
  56→
  57→            fun foo4() {
  58→                fun bar(): Pair<Int, Int> = Pair(1, 2)
  59→
  60→                val (x, y) = bar()
  61→            }
  62→
  63→            val foo5: (Int, Int) -> Int = 42
  64→
  65→            val foo6: (Int, Int) -> Int = { foo, bar -> foo * bar }
  66→            """.trimIndent()
  67→        trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat(code)
  68→            .hasLintViolations(
  69→                LintViolation(1, 29, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
  70→                LintViolation(3, 16, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \">\""),
  71→                LintViolation(6, 9, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"->\""),
  72→                LintViolation(13, 14, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
  73→                LintViolation(16, 20, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
  74→                LintViolation(18, 20, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \")\""),
  75→                LintViolation(18, 42, "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"->\""),
  76→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
  77→    }

 619→    @Test
 620→    fun `Given that a trailing comma is required on declaration site and unused imports do not affect each other`() {
 621→        val code =
 622→            """
 623→            package com.pinterest.ktlint
 624→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.Enum
 625→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.EnumThree
 626→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.EnumTwo
 627→            data class TrailingCommaTest(
 628→                val foo: String,
 629→                val bar: Enum,
 630→                val bar2: EnumTwo,
 631→                val bar3: EnumThree
 632→            )
 633→            """.trimIndent()
 634→        val formattedCode =
 635→            """
 636→            package com.pinterest.ktlint
 637→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.Enum
 638→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.EnumThree
 639→            import com.pinterest.ktlint.enum.EnumTwo
 640→            data class TrailingCommaTest(
 641→                val foo: String,
 642→                val bar: Enum,
 643→                val bar2: EnumTwo,
 644→                val bar3: EnumThree,
 645→            )
 646→            """.trimIndent()
 647→        trailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleAssertThat(code)
 648→            // When running format mode, the rules are first executed in parallel to find linting errors. In this
 649→            // process, no unused import are found because the trailing comma is not yet added to variable "bar3". Then
 650→            // in the next stage the rules are run consecutively. Now the trailing comma rule is adding a trailing comma
 651→            // after the type of variable "bar3". When the no-unused-import rule runs after the trailing-comma rule, it
 652→            // was incorrectly seen as part of the type of variable "bar3" and a reference "EnumThree," (with the
 653→            // trailing comma was added) which in turn resulted in not recognizing that the import of EnumThree actually
 654→            // was used.
 655→            .addAdditionalRuleProvider { NoUnusedImportsRule() }
 656→            .withEditorConfigOverride(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY to true)
 657→            .hasLintViolation(9, 24, "Missing trailing comma before \")\"")
 658→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 659→    }
